title: Public reading layout with section/page/tab navigation
description: Build the dedicated public-facing layout (visually and structurally separate from the admin panel) with a persistent sidebar/nav for sections/pages/tabs and a main content area rendering the current page (via TASK-50's renderer).
acceptanceCriteria: Public layout is a separate app shell/route tree from the admin panel — no admin chrome (toolbar, edit controls) leaks into the public view
Sidebar navigation lists all sections and their pages (tabs shown within a page's own header once a page is open), reflecting the current published structure
Navigating between pages updates the main content area via client-side routing (no full reload) where feasible
Current page/section is visually highlighted in the navigation
URL scheme is human-readable and stable (e.g. `/docs/:sectionSlug/:pageSlug/:tabTitle-or-id`), usable for direct linking/bookmarking

steps.description: Decide and set up the public app's routing structure
steps.details: Add a public route tree (could be a separate build target or a route prefix within the existing admin panel app, served at a different base path — choose based on TASK-6's Dockerfile serving setup) with routes for the doc home, a section/page/tab path, and a 404.

steps.description: Add a public sections/pages listing query
steps.details: Add `sections.listPublic`/`pages.listPublicBySection` (publicProcedure, no auth) returning the structure needed for navigation — reuse TASK-18/20's data but expose via unauthenticated procedures, excluding any admin-only fields.
Create `apps/admin/src/features/public/PublicSidebar.tsx` rendering the section/page tree with active-route highlighting.

steps.description: Wire the page route to TASK-50's renderer
steps.details: The page route resolves `sectionSlug`/`pageSlug` to a `pageId`, fetches its published tabs, and renders the selected tab's content via `PageRenderer` (TASK-50).

Using Playwright, unauthenticated: land on the public doc home, navigate via sidebar into a section → page → tab, confirm the URL updates and the correct content renders, and confirm no admin-only UI (edit buttons, publish button) is present anywhere on the page.

technicalNotes: Only sections/pages/tabs that have at least one published revision should arguably appear in public navigation — decide whether to hide never-published pages from the public sidebar entirely or show them with the 'not published yet' state from TASK-50; the former is the cleaner default for a professional-feeling doc site.
